Do not paste the URL from a rich text source. Type the URL manually, or paste into a plain text editor (Notepad, VS Code) first to strip formatting. Ensure all quotes are straight ASCII double quotes (").

 

CMD

# Wrong — curly quotes from copy-paste (looks fine, breaks silently)

curl -X POST “https://...authentication.../oauth/token”

 

# Correct — straight ASCII double quotes

curl -X POST "https://...authentication.../oauth/token"

 

Error 03 of 12    AUTHENTICATION 

Single quotes in Command Prompt

curl: (3) URL rejected: Port number was not a decimal number between 0 and 65535

 

! ROOT CAUSE

Single quotes work as string delimiters in PowerShell and Linux/Mac terminals — but not in Windows Command Prompt. If you copy a curl command that uses single quotes (common in documentation written for Linux) and run it in cmd, Command Prompt interprets the single quote as part of the URL string.

 

✓ THE FIX

Replace all single quotes with double quotes when running curl in Command Prompt. Also replace Linux line continuation (\) with the Windows caret (^).

 

CMD

# Wrong — single quotes and Linux backslash (fails in Windows cmd)

curl -X POST 'https://...authentication.../oauth/token' \

  -H 'Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded'

 

# Correct — double quotes and Windows caret for line continuation

curl -X POST "https://...authentication.../oauth/token" ^

  -H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded"

 

Error 04 of 12    AUTHENTICATION 

Empty response — no output at all

Command exits silently — no error, no response, no JSON

 

! ROOT CAUSE

The & character in client_credentials&client_id=... is interpreted by Command Prompt as a command separator — splitting your command into two separate commands, one of which runs silently.

 

✓ THE FIX

The entire -d value must be inside double quotes so & is treated as literal text. To capture a suppressed large response, redirect to a file: curl ... > output.txt

 

CMD

# Wrong — & outside quotes splits the command

curl -X POST "https://.../oauth/token" -d grant_type=client_credentials&client_id=abc

 

# Correct — entire -d value inside double quotes

curl -X POST "https://.../oauth/token" ^

  -H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" ^

  -d "grant_type=client_credentials&client_id=YOUR_ID&client_secret=YOUR_SECRET"

 

Error 05 of 12    AUTHENTICATION 

401 Unauthorized — missing /oauth/token path

{"error":"unauthorized","error_description":"An Authentication object was not found..."}

 

! ROOT CAUSE

The url field in your AI Core service key JSON is the base authentication domain — it does NOT include the /oauth/token path. Calling the base domain directly returns 401 because you’re not hitting the token endpoint. The SDK handles this automatically — but direct curl calls require you to add it yourself.

 

✓ THE FIX

Always append /oauth/token to the url value from your service key.

 

CMD

# What's in your service key url field (base domain — NOT the token endpoint)

https://your-subdomain.authentication.us10.hana.ondemand.com

 

# What you need to call (append /oauth/token manually)

curl -X POST "https://your-subdomain.authentication.us10.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/token" ^

  -H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" ^

  -d "grant_type=client_credentials&client_id=YOUR_CLIENTID&client_secret=YOUR_SECRET"

 

💡 PRO TIP

A successful token call returns a JSON object containing access_token — a long base64-encoded string. Copy everything between the quotes after "access_token":. Tokens expire after 3600 seconds (1 hour) — generate a fresh one for each session.

 

Error 06 of 12    AUTHENTICATION 

403 RBAC access denied on resource group creation

{"error":{"code":"403","message":"RBAC: access denied"}}

 

! ROOT CAUSE

Your service key does not have aicore_admin scope. A key created without admin scope can generate tokens and list resources but cannot create or modify resource groups. This is not a token expiry issue and not a URL issue.

 

✓ THE FIX

First, check if a resource group already exists — most BTP-provisioned AI Core instances include a default one. If you genuinely need admin scope, recreate the service key with aicore_admin role.

 

CMD

# Step 1: Check if a resource group already exists (10 seconds, saves debugging time)

curl -X GET "https://YOUR_AI_API_URL/v2/admin/resourceGroups" ^

  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_TOKEN"

 

# If a group with status PROVISIONED exists — use it. No need to create.

# To recreate key with admin scope:

# BTP Cockpit → Services → Instances → AI Core → Service Keys

# Delete existing key → Create new key with aicore_admin role included

 

💡 PRO TIP

Most RBAC errors are unnecessary. Check existing resource groups before attempting to create new ones. The GET call above takes 10 seconds and saves significant debugging time.

 

 

  PART 2 — DEPLOYMENT AND CONFIGURATION ERRORS

These errors appear after authentication works. They're harder to diagnose because the symptoms are misleading and the causes are architectural, not syntactic.

 

Error 07 of 12    DEPLOYMENT 

404 on inference call — deployment shows RUNNING

{"error":{"code":"404","message":"Resource not found"}}

 

! ROOT CAUSE

Three common causes: (1) the deployment belongs to a different AI Core instance than your service key (see Error 8); (2) the token expired between the deployments list call and the inference call; (3) the AI-Resource-Group header is missing or pointing to the wrong group.

 

✓ THE FIX

Get a fresh deployments list with a fresh token. Confirm the deployment ID matches exactly, then run the inference call immediately with the same fresh token.

 

CMD

# Step 1: Get fresh list with fresh token

curl -X GET "https://YOUR_AI_API_URL/v2/lm/deployments" ^

  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_FRESH_TOKEN" ^

  -H "AI-Resource-Group: default"

 

# Step 2: Immediately run inference with same token and confirmed ID

curl -X POST "https://YOUR_AI_API_URL/v2/inference/deployments/YOUR_DEPLOYMENT_ID/chat/completions" ^

  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_FRESH_TOKEN" ^

  -H "AI-Resource-Group: default" ^

  -H "Content-Type: application/json" ^

  -d "{\"messages\":[{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":\"Hello\"}],\"max_tokens\":100}"

 

Error 08 of 12    DEPLOYMENT 

Two AI Core instances — wrong service key

404 on RUNNING deployment — consistent with every fresh token

 

! ROOT CAUSE

Many SAP BTP accounts have multiple AI Core service instances. AI Launchpad can connect to multiple instances via a workspace selector. Your service key belongs to Instance A — but your deployment was created using Instance B in Launchpad. Your token authenticates successfully against Instance A, which has no record of the deployment. The 404 is identical to a missing deployment.

 

✓ THE FIX

Check the instance_id label in the resource group to identify which AI Core instance owns it. Option 1 (fastest): get a service key for the correct instance. Option 2: create a fresh deployment in your own instance.

 

CMD — DIAGNOSTIC

# Check 1: AI_API_URL in your service key — identifies your instance

# Check 2: AI Launchpad workspace header — shows which connection is active

 

# Check 3: Resource group instance_id label

curl -X GET "https://YOUR_AI_API_URL/v2/admin/resourceGroups" ^

  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_TOKEN"

# Find instance_id label → compare against your service key URL's instance

# If they don't match — two-instance problem confirmed

 

# Fix: BTP Cockpit → Services → Instances → correct instance → Service Keys → Create

 

Error 09 of 12    CONFIGURATION 

Resource group stuck in Provisioning

500 on creation / status shows Provisioning and never changes

 

! ROOT CAUSE

Your AI Core service plan may not support creating additional resource groups (some SAP internal plans restrict creation to the default group only). Or the AI Core instance has an underlying platform-level provisioning issue.

 

✓ THE FIX

Wait 5 minutes first (new groups typically provision in under 5 minutes). Verify your service plan supports additional groups. Delete and recreate via curl (more reliable than the UI). If still stuck after 15 minutes, raise a support ticket with component CA-ML-AIP.

 

CMD

# Delete stuck resource group

curl -X DELETE "https://YOUR_AI_API_URL/v2/admin/resourceGroups/YOUR_GROUP_ID" ^

  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_TOKEN"

 

# Recreate via curl (more reliable than UI)

curl -X PUT "https://YOUR_AI_API_URL/v2/admin/resourceGroups" ^

  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_TOKEN" ^

  -H "Content-Type: application/json" ^

  -d "{\"resourceGroupId\": \"your-new-group\"}"

 

# Check status after 3 minutes

curl -X GET "https://YOUR_AI_API_URL/v2/admin/resourceGroups/your-new-group" ^

  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_TOKEN"

 

# If still Provisioning after 15 min: raise ticket with component CA-ML-AIP

 

Error 10 of 12    CONFIGURATION 

409 on resource group delete

{"error":{"code":"409","message":"Conflict"}}

 

! ROOT CAUSE

HTTP 409 Conflict means the resource group is still in Provisioning state and actively being worked on by the platform. Deleting it while provisioning is in progress is not permitted.

 

✓ THE FIX

Wait for the provisioning process to reach either PROVISIONED or ERROR status before attempting to delete. If status has been Provisioning over 15 minutes, wait 30 more minutes. Use the curl DELETE rather than the UI for failed resource groups.

 

CMD

# Wait for PROVISIONED or ERROR status, then:

curl -X DELETE "https://YOUR_AI_API_URL/v2/admin/resourceGroups/YOUR_GROUP_ID" ^

  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_TOKEN"

 

Error 11 of 12    CONFIGURATION 

Orchestration vs foundation-models scenario mismatch

Empty response or format error on inference — deployment shows RUNNING

 

! ROOT CAUSE

SAP AI Core has two distinct deployment scenarios — foundation-models and orchestration — that use entirely different inference endpoint formats and request body structures. foundation-models uses /chat/completions; orchestration uses /completion with an orchestration_config wrapper.

 

✓ THE FIX

Check the scenarioId field in your deployments list response, then use the matching endpoint format.

 

CMD

# Check scenarioId in deployments list:

# {"id":"d01e9590...","scenarioId":"orchestration",...}  <- orchestration

# {"id":"df600c1a...","scenarioId":"foundation-models",...}  <- foundation-models

 

# foundation-models: use /chat/completions

curl -X POST "https://YOUR_AI_API_URL/v2/inference/deployments/YOUR_ID/chat/completions" ^

  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_TOKEN" -H "AI-Resource-Group: default" ^

  -d "{\"model\":\"anthropic--claude-3-5-sonnet\",\"messages\":[{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":\"Hello\"}],\"max_tokens\":100}"

 

# Orchestration: use /completion with orchestration_config wrapper

curl -X POST "https://YOUR_AI_API_URL/v2/inference/deployments/YOUR_ID/completion" ^

  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_TOKEN" -H "AI-Resource-Group: default" ^

  -d "{\"orchestration_config\":{\"module_configurations\":{\"llm_module_config\":{\"model_name\":\"anthropic--claude-3-5-sonnet\",\"model_params\":{\"max_tokens\":500}}}},...}"

 

💡 PRO TIP

For most use cases (Python applications, RAG pipelines, direct model access), foundation-models is the correct scenario. Orchestration is for SAP’s managed prompt orchestration service with content filtering and grounding. If you want direct model access, create your configuration with scenarioId: foundation-models.

 

Error 12 of 12    DEPLOYMENT 

AI Launchpad showing wrong instance

Configurations visible in Launchpad but inference fails from curl

 

! ROOT CAUSE

AI Launchpad is a separate BTP subscription connecting to multiple AI Core instances. If you have Launchpad subscriptions in multiple BTP subaccounts, each opens to a different connection by default. You may be creating deployments in Instance A while your service key belongs to Instance B.

 

✓ THE FIX

Check the browser URL when in AI Launchpad — the subdomain identifies which BTP subaccount you’re using. Compare it against the subdomain in your service key’s url field. If they don’t match, open Launchpad from the correct BTP subaccount.

 

DIAGNOSTIC

# Check your Launchpad URL:

https://YOUR-SUBDOMAIN.ai-launchpad.prod.us-east-1.aws.ai-prod.cloud.sap/...

# Compare YOUR-SUBDOMAIN with the subdomain in your service key url field

# If they differ — you're in the wrong Launchpad instance

 

# Fix: BTP Cockpit → correct subaccount → Instances and Subscriptions

#      → SAP AI Launchpad → Open

# Bookmark the correct Launchpad URL for each subaccount separately

 

 

  QUICK REFERENCE — ALL 12 ERRORS

Scan this table first whenever you hit an error. Use the error number to jump directly to the detailed fix above.

 

#

Symptom

Root Cause

Fix

PART 1 — AUTHENTICATION ERRORS

01

ParameterBindingException — -X not recognized

PowerShell curl alias intercepting command

Use cmd or call curl.exe explicitly

02

Port number not decimal (0–65535)

Curly quotes from copy-paste corrupting URL

Type URL manually or paste through Notepad first

03

Same port error with single quotes

Single quotes invalid in Windows cmd

Replace single quotes with double quotes; use ^ not \

04

No output — command exits silently

& in -d value treated as command separator

Keep entire -d value inside double quotes

05

401 — Authentication object not found

Calling base auth URL without /oauth/token

Append /oauth/token to url from service key

06

403 — RBAC access denied

Service key lacks aicore_admin scope

Check existing groups first; recreate key if needed

PART 2 — DEPLOYMENT AND CONFIGURATION ERRORS

07

404 on RUNNING deployment

Expired token or wrong resource group header

Refresh token; confirm AI-Resource-Group header

08

404 despite correct token and group

Deployment in different AI Core instance

Check instance_id label; get service key for correct instance

09

Resource group stuck in Provisioning

Plan restriction or platform issue

Wait 5 min, retry via curl; raise CA-ML-AIP ticket if >15 min

10

409 on resource group delete

Resource group still in active Provisioning

Wait for PROVISIONED or ERROR status before deleting

11

Empty response / format error on inference

Orchestration called with foundation-models format

Check scenarioId; use /completion for orchestration

12

Deployment in Launchpad but not in curl list

Launchpad connected to wrong subaccount

Open Launchpad from correct BTP subaccount

 

 

  THE COMPLETE WORKING FLOW

All 12 errors resolved. The exact three-step sequence to get a model response. Replace all CAPS values with your service key values.

 

Step 1 — Get a Fresh Bearer Token (valid 1 hour)

CMD

curl -X POST "https://YOUR_SUBDOMAIN.authentication.us10.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/token" ^

  -H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" ^

  -d "grant_type=client_credentials&client_id=YOUR_CLIENTID&client_secret=YOUR_SECRET"

 

# Expected response:

{"access_token":"eyJhbGci...very long string...","token_type":"bearer","expires_in":3599}

 

Step 2 — Confirm Deployment ID and Resource Group

CMD

curl -X GET "https://YOUR_AI_API_URL/v2/lm/deployments" ^

  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_TOKEN" ^

  -H "AI-Resource-Group: default"

 

Step 3 — Call the Model (foundation-models scenario)

CMD

curl -X POST "https://YOUR_AI_API_URL/v2/inference/deployments/YOUR_DEPLOYMENT_ID/chat/completions" ^

  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_TOKEN" ^

  -H "AI-Resource-Group: default" ^

  -H "Content-Type: application/json" ^

  -d "{\"model\":\"YOUR_MODEL_NAME\",\"messages\":[{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":\"Hello, are you working?\"}],\"max_tokens\":100}"

 

# Successful response contains choices[0].message.content with the model reply

{"choices":[{"message":{"role":"assistant","content":"Yes, I'm working correctly..."}}],...}

 

 

 

NEXT STEPS

Once your end-to-end connection is proven, move to the generative-ai-hub-sdk for Python integration. It handles token management, deployment ID resolution, and model routing automatically.

SUPPORT ESCALATION

For resource group provisioning failures persisting beyond 15 minutes, raise a ticket with component CA-ML-AIP. Include your AI Core instance ID and the timestamp of the failed creation attempt.

entry.title: Шлях до автономного підприємства | Agent-Led Toolchain Series
entry.content:
Деякі теми заслуговують на більше, ніж одну сесію.
Agent-Led Toolchain Series — це саме такий випадок: шість вебкастів, бізнес- та ІТ-перспективи, реальні кейси — все разом складає цілісну картину того, як агентно-керований підхід SAP веде організації до автономного підприємства.

Серія охоплює повний шлях RISE — від бізнес-стратегії до ІТ-виконання. Кожна сесія будується на попередній, тому рекомендуємо зберегти всі дати одразу.

Час вказано за Києвом | Мова — англійська.

  • 9 липня, 17:00 – 18:00 
    Agent-Led Toolchain Series: Accelerate ERP modernization and realize long-term business value with SAP's agent-led toolchain 
    Дізнайтеся, як організації — зокрема Nestlé — використовують агентно-керований підхід для оркестрації ERP-трансформації з більшою узгодженістю, операційною видимістю та гнучкістю на всьому шляху RISE. Ви дізнаєтеся, як прискорити трансформацію, покращити її видимість і забезпечити вимірювану бізнес-цінність.
  • 16 липня, 10:00 – 11:00 
    Agent-Led Toolchain Series: Orchestrating the Agent-Led Toolchain Across Your RISE Journey – From a Business Perspective 
    ERP-трансформація — це вже не лише технологічна ініціатива, а стратегічний бізнес-шлях. У цій сесії дізнайтеся, як пов'язати бізнес-пріоритети, процеси, дані та трансформаційні активності для більш цілісного та результативного проходження шляху RISE.
  • 22 липня, 10:00 – 11:00 
    Agent-Led Toolchain Series: Orchestrating the Agent-Led Toolchain Across Your RISE Journey – From an IT Perspective (Part 1) 
    ІТ-команди відіграють ключову роль у вирівнюванні бізнес-пріоритетів, технологічних ландшафтів та стратегій виконання. У першій частині ви дізнаєтесь, як покращити узгодженість трансформації, прискорити її виконання та зміцнити управління й гнучкість на шляху RISE.
  • 29 липня, 10:00 – 11:00 
    Agent-Led Toolchain Series: Orchestrating the Agent-Led Toolchain Across Your RISE Journey – From an IT Perspective (Part 2) 
    Продовження попередньої сесії з поглибленим розглядом практичних підходів до модернізації з більшою впевненістю, гнучкістю та довгостроковим бізнес-ефектом.
  • 5 серпня, 10:00 – 11:00 
    Agent-Led Toolchain Series: From Cloud ERP to Autonomous Enterprise – Aligning Business and IT for Continuous Improvement 
    Перехід на S/4HANA — це фундамент, а не фінішна лінія. Щоб розкрити повний потенціал S/4HANA та рухатися до автономного підприємства, організації мають узгодити бізнес і ІТ для підтримки безперервного вдосконалення, швидшого прийняття рішень та більшої операційної гнучкості. У п'ятому вебкасті серії дізнайтеся, як провідні організації виходять за межі міграції ERP — поєднуючи трансформаційні активності, операційну ефективність та безперервні інновації в масштабах підприємства.
  • 11 серпня, 10:00 – 11:00 
    Agent-Led Toolchain Series: Reimagine ERP Transformation with Migration and Modernization Assistants 
    Як AI-асистенти міграції та модернізації переосмислюють підхід до ERP-трансформації? Ця сесія розкриває, як інтелектуальні інструменти SAP допомагають усунути розрив між бізнесом і ІТ, підтримати безперервне вдосконалення та побудувати стійку основу для довгострокової трансформаційної цінності на шляху до автономного підприємства.

Це повна картина трансформації — від першого кроку міграції до автономного підприємства. Переходьте за посиланнями у назвах вебкастів, реєструйтесь заздалегідь і запрошуйте колег.

Стежте та діліться враженнями✨

entry.title: Hello SAP Community! My name is Aditya Sai Rohith Kaki, and this is my journey into SAP
entry.content:
Hi everyone!

I’m Aditya Sai Rohith Kaki, currently pursuing an MBA in Marketing and IT at ICFAI Business School, Hyderabad.

If someone had asked me a few years ago where I’d be today, I probably wouldn’t have imagined introducing myself to a global SAP community.

I started my career in software testing, working with teams at Accenture and Flecks Labs, where I learned the importance of quality, teamwork, and solving problems with data. Later, during my MBA, I had the opportunity to intern with Varun Beverages (PepsiCo). Spending time in the market, interacting with retailers, analyzing sales data, and building dashboards showed me how technology and business come together to make better decisions.

That curiosity eventually brought me to SAP, and I’m excited to keep learning from this amazing community.  

My Journey into SAP Learning

My interest in SAP started with a simple question:

How do businesses manage everything on one platform? That curiosity led me to the SAP University Alliances program, where I attended a hands-on workshop on End-to-End Business Processes for SAP Business Suite. It gave me my first real glimpse into how different business functions come together through SAP.

Wanting to learn more, I went on to complete the SAP S/4HANA Public Sales (SD) certification. Today, I’m excited to continue exploring the SAP ecosystem. Being part of the #HelloSAPCommunity initiative is a milestone I’m truly grateful for, and I look forward to learning, sharing, and growing with this amazing community.

What is your professional life dream?

My dream is simple to become someone who solves real business problems.

Whether that’s as a Business Consultant, Product Manager, or in another role, I want to bridge the gap between business and technology. I enjoy understanding how businesses work and finding smarter ways to improve processes using data and digital solutions. That’s one of the reasons SAP interests me so much.

Where do you see yourself in 5 years?

Five years from now, I hope to be working on large-scale digital transformation projects, helping businesses make better decisions through technology.

I also want to keep learning because technology changes every day, and I believe staying curious is one of the most valuable skills anyone can have.

What was the most fun project you ever participated in and why?

One experience I’ll always remember was my internship at Varun Beverages (PepsiCo).

Instead of sitting behind a desk, I spent weeks visiting retail stores, talking to shop owners, understanding customer preferences, analyzing competitors, and later converting those insights into Power BI dashboards.

It wasn’t just about numbers it was about seeing how small decisions in the market could make a big difference for a business. That experience made me appreciate the power of data-driven decision-making.  

What do you imagine is the future of AI and Enterprise Technology?

I don’t think AI will replace people.

I think it will help people make faster and better decisions.

The future belongs to professionals who understand both business and technology. Platforms like SAP will become even more intelligent, helping companies predict trends, automate repetitive work, and focus more on innovation. I’m excited to be part of that journey.

What is the best lesson your parents taught you?

One lesson my parents have always reminded me of is:

“Stay grounded, keep learning, and never stop putting in honest effort.”

Life doesn’t always go as planned, but consistency and patience eventually open doors. That’s something I try to carry with me every day.

And now I’d love to hear from you!

If you could learn one new skill that could shape your career over the next five years, what would it be and why?

Feel free to share your thoughts in the comments. I’d love to learn from your experiences too.

Looking forward to connecting with everyone in the SAP Community. Here’s to learning, growing, and building something meaningful together!

#HelloSAPCommunity #SAPCommunity #SAPUniversityAlliances #DigitalTransformation #SAP #AI #BusinessTechnology #LearningJourney

entry.title: SAP Community Voice: AI in your Business, SAP UI5 Survey, July Challenges and Summer Events!
entry.content:
Featured Blog Posts 

Integrating SAP Concur with SAP IAG 

Integrating SAP IAG Enterprise Edition with SAP Concur goes beyond the standard setup guides, and this practical blog post by SAP Community member Swapnil Balharpure helps bridge that gap by sharing valuable implementation experience from a real project. The guide walks through the complete integration process, from the initial SAP IAG setup and SAP Concur OAuth configuration to user provisioning, repository synchronization, access risk analysis, and user ID mapping. Along the way, Swapnil highlights important technical considerations, common implementation challenges, mandatory custom fields, provisioning behavior, and current product limitations, offering practical recommendations to help consultants avoid common pitfalls. If you are planning or currently working on an SAP IAG and SAP Concur integration, this blog post is a must read.  

Read Swapnil Blog Post here 

What Partners need to know about the SAP Business AI Platform 

The SAP expert Yella Reddy explains one of the latest changes to the SAP Partner competency framework: since June 30, 2026, the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) competency has officially been renamed to the SAP Business AI Platform (BAIP) competency. While there are no changes to the current competency requirements and no action is required from existing partners, the blog explains why this represents much more than a simple name change. Yella explores how this update reflects SAP's AI-first strategy, what it means for partner positioning, and why organizations should begin aligning their offerings around AI-driven business outcomes, intelligent automation, SAP Joule Studio, and agentic AI use cases. The post also outlines the next milestones planned for October 2026 and January 2027, when the competency framework will begin introducing new specialization structures and updated knowledge requirements. As the author highlights, "the transition from BTP to BAIP is not simply a branding update — it is an early indicator of how SAP is redefining platform value in the era of Business AI." If you are part of the SAP Partner ecosystem, this is an announcement you will not want to miss. 

Read Yella Reddy Blog Post here 

From Coffee Sales to Business Predictions with SAP RPT 

What if forecasting daily sales could be done without training a machine learning model or deploying complex AI infrastructure? In this hands-on blog post, SAP Champion Chandra Bhushan Singh demonstrates how to use SAP RPT, SAP's foundation model for tabular data, together with SAP AI Core, to predict daily coffee shop sales. Using a simple coffee shop scenario, Chandra walks through the complete process—from setting up SAP AI Core and AI Launchpad to building a Jupyter Notebook, preparing historical data, calling the RPT API, and interpreting the predictions. While the example focuses on coffee sales, the same approach can be applied to demand planning, sales forecasting, customer churn, fraud detection, equipment failure prediction, and many other business scenarios. Throughout the guide, he combines technical implementation with a practical business example, and at the end of the blog invites the community to share feedback, suggestions, and ideas for future SAP AI topics. 

Read Chandra Bhushan Blog Post here 

SAP S/4HANA Custom ABAP CDS View to Datasphere 

If you are working with SAP Datasphere data integration, SAP expert Jeet Kapase shares a comprehensive guide on how to replicate data from a custom ABAP CDS view in SAP S/4HANA into SAP Datasphere using Replication Flows with Change Data Capture (CDC). The blog explains the end-to-end setup, including how RMS, APE, and CDC work together, how to prepare a CDC-enabled custom CDS view, configure Replication Flows for Initial Only and Initial + Delta loads, and understand how deltas are captured through logging tables, buffer tables, and background jobs in SAP S/4HANA. Jeet also demonstrates how to validate inserts, updates, and deletes end to end, explains the different Change_Type values used in SAP Datasphere, and shares monitoring guidance, tuning recommendations, and best practices to help teams build reliable CDC-based replication scenarios. 

Read Jeet Kapase Blog Post here 

Community News 

July Developer Challenge  

We are already in Week 2 of the July SAP Developer Challenge, and we are excited to see so many community members actively participating during the first week! This month's four-week challenge invites developers to build a multiplayer Snake & Ladder game using SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) and CAP Status Transition Flows (@flow.status). Throughout the month, participants will progressively build a complete CAP service featuring turn management, dice rolls, snake and ladder logic, SAP Fiori integration, and even support for the CAP MCP Server, allowing AI assistants to interact with the game through natural language. Every week, a new blog post introduces the next challenge, together with implementation guidance and hints, and participants simply submit their solutions as comments on that week's post. The challenge runs throughout July, with Week 2 (July 13–19) focusing on Roll & Move, followed by Multiplayer & Events (July 20–26) and Fiori UI & CAP MCP (July 27–31). If you would like to join, simply follow the weekly blog posts on SAP Community, complete each task, and submit your answer in the comments to participate alongside the community.  

Read the full blog post here for more information on how to participate!  

Ask Me Anything with SAP Champion: July Edition 

As we' have highlighted in the previous edition, SAP Champion Martin Pankraz is taking the lead for this month Ask Me Anything with the SAP Champions series! And we would like to friendly remind you to take this chance to take part and send your questions. Martin is an expert in SAP Security topics, and this is your chance to understand better about securing SAP S/4HANA, RISE, GROW, SAP BTP, identity and access management, integration security, threat detection, or even the emerging challenges around AI agents accessing SAP data. Questions can be submitted anonymously through Microsoft Forms (link here) and in the comments of Martin blog post. Make sure to keep an eye on the SAP Community YouTube channel for Martin's video answering all your questions! 

SAPUI5 Survey: We want to hear from you! 

As AI continues to reshape how enterprise applications are built, used, and experienced, SAP wants to hear directly from the community. There are only a few days left to participate in the SAP UI Technology Strategy Survey (participate here), which remains open until July 19, 2026. In just five minutes, you can share your perspective on topics such as how well SAPUI5 meets your needs today and in the age of AI, the UI experiences you expect to dominate enterprise applications in the coming years, the capabilities that matter most for your future development work, and where you see SAP's UI technology strategy heading. Whether you are a SAPUI5/Fiori developer, UI or UX architect, technical lead, enterprise architect, or IT decision maker, this is your opportunity to help influence the future direction of SAP UI technologies. Make sure to complete the survey before July 19 and let your voice be heard! More details about it here.  

Events 

SAP Stammtisch Bamberg  

If you are in the Bamberg region and looking to connect with your local SAP community, do not miss the next SAP Stammtisch Bamberg on July 30. Formerly known as Stammtisch Oberfranken, this informal meetup is an opportunity to exchange ideas, discuss the latest SAP topics, and network with fellow SAP professionals in a relaxed atmosphere. The community will meet from 6:00 PM at St. GeorgenBräu Bierkeller in Buttenheim, and even if you cannot make it right at the start, you're welcome to join later in the evening. Do not forget to join the Bamberg Location Group to stay informed about future meetups and community activities! Register here. 

Online and Free Event: From Classical SAP to AI Architect 

Looking to explore how Generative AI and SAP BTP are transforming the SAP landscape? The SAP community Nigeria is hosting a virtual session on July 22 at 3:00 PM (WAT) titled "From Classical SAP to AI Architect: Solving Business Bottlenecks with Generative AI and the Adaptive Semantic Extraction Framework on SAP BTP for S/4HANA." Whether you are a classical SAP professional beginning your AI journey or looking to deepen your SAP BTP knowledge, this session will explore what AI and BTP mean for traditional SAP modules and how you can start expanding your skill set. The event will also feature a real-world success story demonstrating how the Adaptive Semantic Extraction Framework was used to automate complex sales orders and solve major operational bottlenecks. The session is free and virtual, so make sure to reserve your spot on the official event page here.  

SAP Inside Track´s in July!

As we mentioned in our last edition, this month, we are excited to have two major SAP Inside Track events taking place! We would like to remind everyone in United States and Brazil, that you still have time to sign up for both SAP Inside Track Columbus Registration and SAP Inside Track Sao Paulo Registration.  

More information: 

On July 25, the first-ever SAP Inside Track Columbus will bring the local SAP community together in Columbus, Ohio, at 200 E Campus View Blvd, Suite 200, for a full day of learning and networking. The event is designed for everyone interested in SAP. Alongside a full day of expert-led sessions, attendees will have the opportunity to connect with fellow SAP professionals from across the Midwest over food, drinks, and discussions. 

The following day, on July 26, SAP Inside Track São Paulo will take place at PUC Marques de Paranaguá – Auditório Marques de Paranaguá, in São Paulo, Brazil. This community-driven event welcomes customers, consultants, developers, architects, and freelancers for a full day of technical sessions presented in Portuguese and English. As with every SAP Inside Track, attendees are encouraged to help shape the agenda and contribute to the community experience.

entry.title: 2026 SAP Quality Awards for Customer Success CEE | Номінуйте ваш SAP-проєкт
entry.content:
Номінаційний період 2026 SAP Quality Awards for Customer Success CEE відкрито — дедлайн 31 серпня 2026 року. Якщо ваша компанія успішно реалізувала SAP-проєкт — номінуйтесь вже сьогодні!

Україна вже на карті переможців
За 2024–2025 роки українські компанії здобули 7 нагород SAP Quality Awards for Customer Success CEE (Central & Eastern Europe).🏆✨

2025 — SAP Quality Awards for Customer Success CEE

  • Оператор ГТС України — Business Transformation – Enterprise Solutions (також отримав спеціальну відзнаку SAP Quality Director Award)
  • Novus Ukraine — Business Transformation – Enterprise Solutions
  • Metinvest — Business Transformation – Functional Transformation
  • Business Evolution — Rapid Time to Value

2024 — SAP Quality Awards for Customer Success CEE

  • Дарниця — Business Transformation – Corporate Solutions
  • Національна служба здоров’я України (НСЗУ) — Business Transformation – Corporate Solutions
  • Metinvest — Business Transformation – Functional Transformation

Це не випадковість — це підтвердження того, що українські підприємства реалізують проєкти світового рівня навіть в умовах воєнного часу. Хто буде наступним у 2026? 

Що таке SAP Quality Awards for Customer Success?
Престижна нагорода SAP, яка відзначає клієнтів за видатні результати впровадження SAP-рішень. Переможці отримують:

  • Визнання в SAP-спільноті та публічне висвітлення в медіа і пресі
  • Участь у церемонії нагородження
  • Офіційний логотип переможця для використання в комунікаціях компанії

Три категорії для номінації
1) Business Transformation — масштабні трансформаційні проєкти
Два з трьох критеріїв: понад 500 користувачів | понад 6 місяців | понад 900 людино-днів

2) Rapid Time to Value — швидкі та ефективні впровадження
Два з трьох критеріїв: менше 600 користувачів | менше 7 місяців | менше 1 000 людино-днів

3) Sustainability — проєкти із вимірюваним впливом на сталий розвиток
Використання SAP Sustainability Portfolio + вимірюваний вплив на Цілі сталого розвитку ООН

Хто може подати заявку?

  • Будь-яка організація з успішним впровадженням будь-якого SAP-продукту
  • Проєкт запущений у продуктивну експлуатацію від 2 до 18 місяців до дати подачі заявки
  • Одна компанія може подати заявки в кількох категоріях — за умови, що кожна заявка стосується окремого SAP-проєкту

Як подати заявку?

  1. Відкрийте анкету з номінаційного порталу
  2. Заповніть усі питання англійською або німецькою мовою
  3. Подайте номінацію онлайн до 31 серпня 2026

Детальна інформація та посилання на номінаційний портал: SAP Quality Awards for Customer Success

Якщо ваш проєкт відповідає критеріям — не зволікайте.🏆
Нехай 2026 додасть ще більше українських імен до списку переможців. 🇺🇦

entry.title: Random Day Surprise Is Back and You May Have Just Earned Our Newest Badge!
entry.content:
SAP Community is always looking for fun and meaningful ways to recognize the contributions that make this site so valuable. Today, we’re thrilled to reintroduce a fan favorite program, with a fresh twist.

Random Day Surprise is back!

Random Day Surprise is a badge program designed to celebrate the everyday moments of engagement that keep our community thriving. On select (and secret!) days throughout the year, we’ll recognize members who jump in and contribute! It could be by asking a thoughtful question, sharing expertise, writing a blog, reacting to a post, or helping another member.

You won’t know when the next surprise day will be and that’s half the fun. 

Meet the Mascot: No Drama Llama
Every great program needs a great mascot, and ours is here to keep things light, friendly, and a little bit silly: No Drama Llama

Each Random Day Surprise badge will feature No Drama Llama, paired with playful art inspired by the day itself. Expect creative, collectible designs that reflect both the spirit of the occasion and the personality of our community.

While we fondly wave goodbye to our beloved chicken mascot from the original program,

we’re excited to welcome the llama into the spotlight, inspired by the No Drama Llama who made a memorable appearance at last year’s SAP TechEd.

How It Works
There is nothing to sign up for. Just keep doing what you already do:

  • Ask questions
  • Share expertise
  • Write blogs
  • Answer others
  • React and engage

On select secret days, your contributions will unlock a surprise badge featuring No Drama Llama. Badges will appear on your profile the following day, no action is needed on your end. To check your earned badges, click the My Community Profile link from the avatar flyout menu in the header of community.sap.com.  

You’ll see your most recently earned badges in the top right corner and you can see all the badges earned from the View all badges link.

Activity counts if it happens on the recognized day, plus or minus six hours (based on UTC), giving all members of our global community a fair shot no matter what their time zone.

Surprise #1: World Emoji Day (July 17)
We kicked things off with our first recognition for the new program tied to World Emoji Day, which this year fell on on Friday, July 17.
World Emoji Day       World Emoji Day Bonus

If you engaged in SAP Community on July 17, or within the six hour window before or after, take a look at your profile, where you should find a No Drama Llama greeting you.


Whether you reacted with a like, shared insights, asked a question, or helped solve someone’s problem, your participation counted. Thank you for showing up and making the community what it is. We also awarded a special bonus badge for anyone who used an emoji in a post during the same time frame: Random Day Surprise - World Emoji Day Bonus

What’s Next?
Random Day Surprise will pop up throughout the rest of the year, sometimes tied to global moments, sometimes just because. We won’t reveal the dates in advance, but we might drop a few hints along the way… Here are a few hints of days to watch out for:

  • Big ideas and the people behind them
  • The things that help us grow
  • Small joys that keep us going
  • Quiet contributions that make a difference


Can you guess any of them? What day would you love to see celebrated in SAP Community? Drop your ideas and guesses in the comments below.

entry.title: SAP CodeJam HANA AI at Code:Connect 2026-07 Recap
entry.content:
During Code:Connect 2026, we held an SAP CodeJam event in St. Leon-Rot, Germany. The event focused on exploring the foundations of AI applications with the SAP HANA Cloud: Vector Engine and Knowledge Graph Engine.

Thanks to all participants who joined, and also to @ChristophMorgen for his presentation!

 

 I hope exercises were helpful, insightful, and -- sometimes -- entertaining 🤓

This CodeJam was an introductory for the HANA Tech Con happening the same week, where I had the pleasure to co-present with my fellow dev advocates (from the image above): @noravonthenen 

...and @thomas_jung 

Would you like to host such an SAP CodeJam in your location?
